---
title: Films and Documentaries
---

Feature-length documentaries and narrative films used across the school's themes — standalone works you would screen start to finish, often paired with a reading or as the centerpiece of a screening followed by discussion. For shorter online explainers, lectures, and video essays, see [[videos-and-lectures|Videos and Lectures]].

> This table is generated automatically from the `resources:` frontmatter on each theme's lens files. Do not edit it by hand — add or change a resource in the relevant lens file and run `npm run resources`.

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED: resources — edit lens frontmatter, then run `npm run resources` -->

## A note on selection

Films can do work that text cannot. They also carry their own ideology, framings, and omissions. Sessions built around films benefit from explicit discussion of what the film does and does not show.

## A note on access

Many documentaries are free online (PBS, the Internet Archive, filmmakers' own sites); others require purchase or library access. The access column notes the situation where known.

## Status

This index is generated from theme content. To add a film, add it to the relevant theme lens and re-run the generator. Contributions welcome via pull request.
